title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two colleagues talking about working from home.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you working from home tomorrow? || Woman: Yes, but only in the morning. I can answer emails easily from home, and it's quieter there. [8]I need to come into the office after lunch because the printer at home is broken and I have to prepare documents for a meeting[/8]. || Man: I can help you print them when you arrive. || Woman: Anyway, I’ll let you know what happens, because it is one of those small things that seems easy until you have to deal with it yourself.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you working from home tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but only in the morning. I can answer emails easily from home, and it's quieter there. I need to come into the office after lunch because the printer at home is broken and I have to prepare documents for a meeting.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I can help you print them when you arrive.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Anyway, I’ll let you know what happens, because it is one of those small things that seems easy until you have to deal with it yourself.

questions.question: Why will the woman go to the office tomorrow?

options.A: She needs to print some documents.
options.B: Her home is too noisy for work.
options.C: She has to answer important emails.

questions.explanation: She says she must go to the office because her home printer is broken and she needs to prepare documents.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking about a hotel breakfast.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Was the hotel breakfast good? || Man: Mostly. There was plenty of fruit, and the coffee was excellent. I didn't try the cooked breakfast because the queue was long. [9]The only thing missing was fresh bread; they just had dry toast in packets[/9]. || Woman: That's disappointing. Good bread makes breakfast much better. || Woman: Still, I’m glad we talked about it, because it helps to hear another opinion before making a final decision about something like this.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was the hotel breakfast good?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Mostly. There was plenty of fruit, and the coffee was excellent. I didn't try the cooked breakfast because the queue was long. The only thing missing was fresh bread; they just had dry toast in packets.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's disappointing. Good bread makes breakfast much better.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Still, I’m glad we talked about it, because it helps to hear another opinion before making a final decision about something like this.

questions.question: What did the man dislike about the breakfast?
questions.explanation: He says fresh bread was missing and there was only dry toast in packets.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a theatre workshop.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you going to the theatre workshop next month? || Man: I wanted to, but it's full now. My sister went last year and loved it. [10]She said the best part was meeting the actors after the performance and asking them questions[/10]. || Woman: That sounds brilliant. Maybe they'll run another workshop later in the year. || Woman: I suppose it is a good lesson for next time, and at least I know what to check before I make any plans again.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you going to the theatre workshop next month?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I wanted to, but it's full now. My sister went last year and loved it. She said the best part was meeting the actors after the performance and asking them questions.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds brilliant. Maybe they'll run another workshop later in the year.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suppose it is a good lesson for next time, and at least I know what to check before I make any plans again.

questions.question: What did the man's sister enjoy most about the workshop?
questions.explanation: He says his sister enjoyed meeting the actors after the performance and asking questions.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a recipe.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did that new recipe work well? || Woman: Yes, in the end. It looked complicated, but most steps were easy. I nearly forgot to add the lemon, though. [11]What really improved the dish was cooking the vegetables slowly instead of rushing them[/11]. || Man: I'll remember that when I try it. || Woman: Anyway, it was useful to talk it through, because now the whole situation feels a bit clearer than it did before. || Woman: That should make the choice easier now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did that new recipe work well?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, in the end. It looked complicated, but most steps were easy. I nearly forgot to add the lemon, though. What really improved the dish was cooking the vegetables slowly instead of rushing them.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I'll remember that when I try it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Anyway, it was useful to talk it through, because now the whole situation feels a bit clearer than it did before.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That should make the choice easier now.

questions.question: What made the recipe successful?
questions.explanation: She says cooking the vegetables slowly was what really improved the dish.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a college open day.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did the open day help you choose a college? || Man: It did. The building wasn't as modern as the other one I visited, but the students were friendly. [12]The art teacher showed me the studios and explained exactly what we'd do in the first year[/12]. || Woman: That sounds useful. You needed clear information about the course. || Woman: I’ll remember that for the future, because small details like that can make a normal day much easier or much more difficult.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did the open day help you choose a college?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It did. The building wasn't as modern as the other one I visited, but the students were friendly. The art teacher showed me the studios and explained exactly what we'd do in the first year.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds useful. You needed clear information about the course.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ll remember that for the future, because small details like that can make a normal day much easier or much more difficult.

questions.question: Why was the open day useful for the man?

options.B: He learnt detailed information about the course.
options.C: He met a lot of new students.

questions.explanation: He says the teacher explained what students do in the first year, giving clear course information.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a shopping centre.
extracts.audio_script: Man: What do you think of the new shopping centre? || Woman: It's convenient because the parking is free, and there are lots of shops. [13]But I don't like the way it's designed. I keep going the wrong way because all the entrances look the same[/13]. || Man: I got lost there too. They need better signs. || Woman: That’s true. I think I know what to do now, and I’m glad I didn’t make the decision too quickly.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What do you think of the new shopping centre?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It's convenient because the parking is free, and there are lots of shops. But I don't like the way it's designed. I keep going the wrong way because all the entrances look the same.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I got lost there too. They need better signs.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That’s true. I think I know what to do now, and I’m glad I didn’t make the decision too quickly.

questions.question: What does the woman dislike about the shopping centre?
questions.explanation: She says she keeps going the wrong way because the entrances look the same.
